compressed_prompt: night-sector commandos story, no caps, no 'coop'
response:
here’s a rough outline for your night-sector commando story—no caps, no "coop," just raw tension and shadows:

---

**title: blackout protocol**

the mission brief came in fragments, static hissing over encrypted comms. *"target: abandoned refinery, sector 7. intel suggests hostiles using it as a staging point. no reinforcements. no extraction. you’re ghosts."*

the team moved like smoke—four operators, faces smeared with grease, gear stripped down to essentials. no patches, no names. just the mission.

first contact was a whisper. a guard, cigarette glowing in the dark, rifle slung low. one of the commandos slipped behind him, blade flashing once. the body hit the ground before the ember died.

inside, the refinery was a maze of rusted pipes and flickering emergency lights. shadows stretched like fingers. the team split—two went left, two right. no radios, just hand signals. trust was the only comms they needed.

then the ambush hit.

muzzle flashes lit the corridor like lightning. the commandos scattered, returning fire in controlled bursts. one took a round to the shoulder, went down hard. his teammate dragged him behind cover, pressed a bandage into the wound. *"stay quiet. we’re not done."*

the fight turned into a hunt. the commandos moved like predators, using the dark as cover. one by one, the hostiles fell—silenced pistols, garrotes, the occasional suppressed rifle shot. no mercy, no witnesses.

at the heart of the refinery, they found the intel—a server humming in a locked room. the team’s hacker went to work, fingers flying over the keyboard. *"got it. uploading now. thirty seconds."*

then the alarms blared.

the refinery erupted in gunfire. the commandos fought their way back, covering each other’s retreat. the hacker took a bullet to the leg, but kept typing. *"almost… there…"*

they made it to the extraction point—a black ops chopper, rotors already spinning. the team piled in, the hacker collapsing into the seat. the chopper lifted off, disappearing into the night.

back at base, the intel was analyzed. the mission was a success. but no one celebrated. no medals, no debrief. just the next target, waiting in the dark.
